_comment: Windows memory-forensics golden for MemLabs Lab 3 - The Evil's Den.
The public prompt states that a malicious script encrypted secret information and that steghide is required. This is CTF-style ground truth, not a full incident answer key.

findings.description: Memory image contains evidence of a malicious script that encrypted secret information
findings.artifact_hint: Volatility command line, consoles, process, or filescan evidence for the script

findings.description: First half of the flag or secret can be recovered from memory before extracting the second half
findings.artifact_hint: Recovered memory strings or dumped files; flag value intentionally omitted

findings.description: Second half of the flag requires steganography extraction with steghide from a recovered artifact
findings.artifact_hint: Recovered image/file from memory, followed by steghide extraction
